source_paper_title: On Time-Consistent Equilibrium Stopping Under Aggregation of Diverse Discount Rates
context:
Section 5.1 (Geometric Brownian motion), near Example 2 / Remark 5.3, around page 28 (PDF pp. 27–28 of the parsed text)

As a sharp contrast, in our framework with a general attitude function \(\varphi(x)\), it is an appealing problem to find a general characterization of the optimal equilibrium if it exists, which will be left for our future research.
title: General characterization of the optimal equilibrium in time-inconsistent stopping with nonlinear aggregation over heterogeneous discount rates
latex:
Given a filtered probability space \((\Omega,\mathcal F,(\mathcal F_t)_{t\ge 0},\mathbb P)\) supporting a one-dimensional time-homogeneous diffusion \(X^x=(X^x_t)_{t\ge 0}\) with initial state \(X^x_0=x\in\mathbb R\), let \(\mathcal T\) be the set of all \((\mathcal F_t)\)-stopping times. Let \(g:\mathbb R\to\mathbb R\) be a (measurable) payoff function and let \(\rho\) be an \([0,\infty)\)-valued random variable, independent of \(X^x\), with distribution function \(F_\rho\). Let \(\varphi:\mathbb R\to\mathbb R\) be a strictly increasing (not necessarily linear) aggregation/attitude function.

For any Borel set \(R\subseteq\mathbb R\), define the hitting time
\[
\tau(x,R):=\inf\{t>0: X^x_t\in R\}
\]
(with the convention \(\inf\emptyset=\infty\)), and define the aggregated continuation value
\[
J(x,R):=\int_{0}^{\infty} \varphi\Big(\mathbb E\big[e^{-r\tau(x,R)}\,g\big(X^x_{\tau(x,R)}\big)\big]\Big)\,dF_\rho(r).
\]
Define the induced value of stopping now versus continuing under \(R\) as
\[
V(x,R):=\max\{\varphi(g(x)),\,J(x,R)\}.
\]
Let \(\Theta\) be the policy-improvement operator on Borel sets defined by
\[
\Theta(R):=\{x\in R: \varphi(g(x))\ge J(x,R)\}\cup R,
\]
and call \(R\) a (mild) equilibrium if \(\Theta(R)=R\). Let \(\mathcal E\) denote the set of all equilibria.

Problem: Provide general conditions on \(X^x\), \(g\), \(F_\rho\), and \(\varphi\) (allowing \(\varphi\) to violate the sufficient condition \(x\mapsto \varphi'(x)\,x\) increasing) under which an optimal equilibrium exists, and if it exists, characterize an optimal equilibrium \(R^\star\in\mathcal E\) satisfying
\[
V(x,R^\star)\ge V(x,R)\quad\text{for all }x\in\mathbb R\text{ and all }R\in\mathcal E.
\]
In particular, determine whether \(R^\star\) can be expressed through an explicit set operation on \(\mathcal E\) (e.g., an intersection/union or other canonical selection), or via a tractable analytic characterization (e.g., a barrier-type characterization) in the general nonlinear aggregation setting.
